notes: Most verbose output of all runs (31k tokens) but missed 2 key assertions
The 'final closeout block' assertion is the most discriminating — with_skill passes 3/3, without_skill passes 0/3. This is the clearest value-add of the skill.
Priorities check assertion is also discriminating — without_skill failed it on eval 2 (casual phrasing), suggesting the skill is needed to enforce the full workflow on ambiguous prompts.

Decision extraction is NOT discriminating — both configs pass it consistently. The without_skill baseline naturally extracts decisions when the user mentions them. The skill adds the standardized format.
Without-skill runs used more tokens on average (25k vs 23k) and were slower, suggesting the skill actually streamlines execution.
Eval 2 (casual phrasing) is the toughest test — without_skill dropped to 50% pass rate vs 100% with_skill. The skill is most valuable when the user's prompt is ambiguous.
